methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Late Stage → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $5.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Chips & Semiconductors (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 12 same-sector peers exist in Chips & Semiconductors, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 23.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Rapidus is a late-stage semiconductor company that has raised $5.0B+ to date. The relevant comp universe consists of 3 public semiconductor peers (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private peers in the Chips & Semiconductors sector, though none are sufficiently comparable on stage and valuation tier to support peer-median analysis.

Given data constraints, valuation relies on stage-progression methodology. Using total capital raised ($5.0B) as a proxy for enterprise value and applying a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical of late-stage to next-stage advancement yields $6.0B. This approach assumes capital deployment correlates with value creation and that the company has not significantly destroyed or created value relative to its raise trajectory.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $5.1B to $6.9B, with a median of $6.0B. This range reflects uncertainty around the stage-progression multiplier (which may vary materially by capital deployment efficiency and IP maturity) and the absence of direct peer anchoring. Public comps show a 23.4x median revenue multiple across semiconductors, but without disclosed target revenue, this metric cannot be applied. The primary uncertainty is whether Rapidus' capital intensity and pre-revenue or early-revenue status will sustain or contract its valuation relative to deployment. Additional disclosure of revenue, gross margins, and technical milestones would materially refine this estimate.
